blocks.content: He wasn't upset. He just rode fast because that was the thing that helped.
He went past the field without looking at it.
At home he sat at the kitchen table and his mom came in from the other room and looked at him and didn't say anything, just put a glass of water in front of him, and he looked at the table.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: When you — when there's something you should say. How do you know when you've waited too long.
His mom sat down across from him.

blocks.speaker: Kit's Mom
blocks.content: I know that girl. I've known her since she was eight years old. And I know you.
Kit looked at the glass of water.

blocks.speaker: Kit's Mom
blocks.content: But Kit — you might be getting close.
